retrieved_contexts: Route decision decides current_article versus evidence lookup.
Time to first token and stream duration are core AI latency measures.
Follow-up and citation-open events are quality proxy signals.
answer: Observe the route decision, retrieved context count, time to first token, stream duration, and follow-up behavior to understand a streaming chat pipeline.

retrieved_contexts: Redis hit rate dropping is a direct cache quality signal.
Bigkeys and hotkeys can make notebook detail reads slower.
Node CPU helps rule out host saturation.
answer: Look at hit rate, evictions, slowlog growth, fragmentation, and whether bigkeys or hotkeys are increasing when notebook detail latency gets worse.

retrieved_contexts: Search, Import, and AI were defined as the highest-priority business chains.
Latency and quality regressions on those paths matter most.
Peripheral CRUD endpoints can remain lower priority.
answer: They are the core business chains, so latency, failures, and quality regressions there matter more than peripheral CRUD endpoints.

retrieved_contexts: Benchmark runners can export Prometheus textfiles.
Node exporter textfile collection can pick up offline metrics.
Grafana can then plot the benchmark gauges over time.
answer: Offline benchmark results can be exported as Prometheus textfiles, collected by node_exporter, and then visualized in Grafana as time series.
